candidates.title: Professional Photoshop Editor Needed for Ongoing eCommerce Product Editing & Color Swapping
candidates.brief: We require FAST turnaround on all work. We are looking for a highly skilled and reliable Photoshop professional for ongoing eCommerce product photo manipulation. While the technical requirements of this job are relatively basic, the final execution must be flawless. We frequently need to update our existing product catalog (e.g., for our brand, by changing colors on specific product parts without having to reshoot the images. Examples of Work Needed: Changing the colors of text/design on product labels. Swapping colors on hardware (e.g., winch hooks, accessories) from existing photos. General eCommerce product image manipulation. The Catch: You must be able to change these colors without ruining the original image. The lighting, shadows, and textures must remain completely natural. We cannot accept sloppy masking, flat colors, or unrealistic edits. It might be basic Photoshop work, but w

candidates.title: Bold Men’s Wellness Ad Creatives
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for layered/retouched assets and video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I need three 1080×1080 static images and one 15-30 sec video that feel bold and energetic, built specifically to drive direct sales on both Facebook and Instagram. You’ll receive high-resolution product photos, the landing page URL, brand guidelines, and a few competitor examples; your job is to turn them into premium-looking ads that stop the scroll, highlight benefits fast, and close with a strong call to action. You should already know the nuances of eCommerce and D2C creative testing—visual hierarchy that works in the Meta feed, safe-zone text placement, and thumb-stopping motion for short-form video. If you’ve produced for wellness, supplements, or similar niches, that background will help you nail the messaging style more quickly. Deliverables • 3 static ad images (JPG/PNG + layered PSD/AI/Canva) • 1 short ad video (MP4 + project file) • Designs formatted for Facebook & Instagram p

candidates.title: UGC Ad Video Editor for Meta, Google
candidates.tools: After Effects · Premiere Pro — for layered/retouched assets and video/motion editing
candidates.brief: **Title:** UGC Video Editor Needed for Meta & Google Ads (Short Form Video Specialist) **Job Description:** We are looking for a skilled **UGC Video Editor** who can create high-converting advertising videos for our clients and brands. ### Responsibilities: * Edit UGC-style videos for Meta (Facebook & Instagram) Ads and Google Ads. * Combine multiple images, short video clips, product photos, screenshots, and stock footage into engaging ad creatives. * Add captions, motion graphics, transitions, hooks, and call-to-action elements. * Create short-form videos (15 sec, 30 sec, 60 sec). * Optimize videos for higher CTR, engagement, and conversions. ### Requirements: * Experience editing UGC and direct-response ad creatives. * Knowledge of Meta Ads and Google Ads video requirements. * Ability to create scroll-stopping hooks in the first 3 seconds. * Experience with CapCut, Premiere Pro, After

candidates.title: Dynamic Gym Video Editor
candidates.tools: After Effects · Premiere Pro — for video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I run a fitness channel and I need an editor who can turn my raw workout footage into energetic, scroll-stopping content. Expect plenty of burpees, heavy lifts, and explosive movement—your job is to match that energy on screen. Transitions and effects I like my cuts fast and my transitions dynamic and flashy. Each video should feel like one continuous burst of action, supported by clean text overlays for reps or tips, crisp slow-motion highlights, and a consistent color grade that really makes the sweat and steel pop. Workflow Every week I will send over 3–4 video sets. First, you will receive a small batch of clips as a trial; show me your approach and I will let you know if we are a fit. Once approved, we roll into a monthly arrangement with a steady flow of footage. Turnaround needs to keep pace with my posting schedule, so reliable delivery is essential. candidates.title: Social Media Shorts Editing
candidates.tools: After Effects · Premiere Pro — for video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I have a steady stream of vertical videos that need to be transformed into polished Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and Facebook clips. Each raw clip arrives between 15-60 seconds; once in your hands, it should come back looking ready for a brand feed: clean color grade, well-paced cuts, tasteful transitions, on-brand text animations, and copyright-safe background music that feels fresh rather than stock. Here’s what matters most to me: • Flow – every transition should feel intentional and keep the viewer hooked from first frame to last. • Text treatment – captions and callouts need to pop without overpowering the footage and must remain legible on small screens. • Sound – I’ll pass reference tracks; you handle leveling, sync, and any subtle SFX that punch up engagement. • Speed – turnarounds within 24–48 hours per edit are ideal so the content calendar stays on track. • Consistency – e

replacement_note: GEN-FREE replacement for a removed generative-expand task. NO image_generative_expand, NO generative fill/outpaint, NO upscale. O8 stock-hero = license a correctly-sized stock image + crop/reframe + place logo (NOT expand a small image).
